title: Robots are about to overtake armed soldiers as the deciders of war
description: Uncrewed ground vehicles have already been tested for defending the front line by the Ukrainian military. Despite their limitations, these remotely controlled robots could be the deciding factor in many conflicts
author: David Hambling

title: Iron Age Britons may have removed the brains of the dead
description: Scrape marks inside a skull and sharpened limb bones in a set of remains found in Scotland may be evidence of unusual Iron Age funerary rituals
author: Chris Simms

title: Frozen squirrel scat preserves ancient DNA from hundreds of species
description: A complex ecosystem of woolly mammoths, bison, horses and big cats has been elucidated by studying the faeces of small rodents that probably ate the bigger animals
author: Chris Simms

title: Dinosaur-killing asteroid impact site stayed hot for millions of years
description: Drill cores at the impact site of the Chicxulub asteroid show evidence that, alongside widespread destruction, the collision created a vast underground ecosystem filled with hot water that sheltered microbial life
author: James Woodford

title: Wildlife thrives in solar farm built on restored peatland
description: A diverse range of bird species has been recorded at a solar park on rewetted peatland in Germany, suggesting that combining energy generation with habitat restoration could benefit biodiversity, the climate and the economy
author: Alec Luhn

title: Can Apple and Google stop children from sharing explicit images?
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has warned tech firms, including Apple and Google, that they must voluntarily implement tools to stop children sharing explicit images, but experts warn this is easier said than done
author: Chris Stokel-Walker
